summary: Do not ship B-1 yet. S-1 and S-2 are structurally resolved, but the committed seam cannot host B-2’s required deterministic witnesses without an unplanned visibility change or weaker tests.

findings.title: B-2’s deterministic test seam is split across private sibling modules
findings.body: `run_once_at` is private to `reconcile.rs`, while the hook fields needed to construct B-2’s prescribed barrier choreography are private to sibling module `pins.rs`. Tests under `pins.rs` can construct `Hooks` but cannot call the injected-time reconciler; tests under `reconcile.rs` have the inverse limitation. B-2 repeatedly requires both facilities in the same deterministic witnesses, so it cannot compose onto B-1 as planned without widening production visibility, adding an unplanned bridge, or weakening the recorded injected-time choreography. This should be corrected while the seam is still cheap to change.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicitly test-only injected-time reconcile wrapper with visibility limited to `store`, callable from `pins.rs` tests. Keep `run_once_at`, protection state, and all seven hook fields private in production.

next_steps: Add the test-only cross-module bridge to B-1.
Confirm B-2’s planned barrier tests can access both hook construction and injected time without expanding production APIs.
Repeat the structure review; do not re-run machine-owned tests in this gate.
